name: Senda del Aguacae (PRC-BU-160) — circular desde Fuentenebro
region_intro: Hiking around Fuentenebro offers trails through varied landscapes, including river gorges and natural park areas. The region is characterized by its riverbanks and streams, providing diverse environments for outdoor exploration. Hikers can expect routes that traverse both open terrain and areas with natural features like the Hoces del Riaza. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making the area accessible for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Fuentenebro The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's river gorges. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hoces del Riaza — circular desde Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela: Natural Park Hoces del río Riaza, an easy 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail offers views of the Riaza river and its surrounding natural environment. Local hikers also love the Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo: Parque natural Hoces del río Riaza, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through the Natural Park Hoces del río Riaza, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Fuentenebro is defined by river gorges, natural park areas, and riverbank paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Fuentenebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuentenebro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How difficult are the hiking trails around Fuentenebro?
A: Fuentenebro offers a range of hiking trails suitable for various experience levels. You'll find 7 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 1 more challenging option, ensuring there's something for everyone from casual walkers to experienced hikers.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Fuentenebro?
A: Yes, Fuentenebro has several family-friendly options. Many of the 7 easy routes are suitable for families, often following riverbanks with gentle elevation changes. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153664073/hoces-del-riaza-circular-desde-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela-natural-park-hoces-del-rio-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Riaza — circular desde Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 4.6-mile (7.4 km) path offering scenic views of the Riaza river.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Fuentenebro?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in the Fuentenebro region, especially within natural park areas like the Hoces del Riaza. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check specific park regulations if available.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Fuentenebro?
A: Yes, Fuentenebro features several excellent circular routes. Popular choices include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036582116/natural-park-of-the-hoces-de-rio-riaza-circular-from-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt;, which explores river gorges, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153647135/senda-de-la-ermita-de-casuar-circular-desde-valdevacas-de-montejo-parque-natural-hoces-del-rio-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo&lt;/a&gt;, leading through the Natural Park Hoces del río Riaza.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Fuentenebro?
A: The Fuentenebro area is rich in natural beauty, particularly within the Hoces del Riaza Natural Park. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of the Hoces del Río Riaza&lt;/a&gt; and observe diverse birdlife at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejo Bird of Prey Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails follow the scenic Riaza River.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or interesting places to visit near Fuentenebro's trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural landscapes, you can discover historical sites. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haza and its castle&lt;/a&gt;, a charming village with a rich past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5623538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hornuez&lt;/a&gt; also offers a glimpse into the region's cultural heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Fuentenebro?
A: The best times to hike in Fuentenebro are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, while winters can be chilly, though still offering opportunities for crisp, clear walks.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Fuentenebro?
A: Parking for hiking trails in Fuentenebro is generally available in the nearby villages that serve as trailheads, such as Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela or Valdevacas de Montejo. Look for designated parking areas or street parking within these communities.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see on Fuentenebro's hiking trails?
A: Given the region's focus on natural parks and river environments, you have a good chance of spotting various wildlife. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejo Bird of Prey Refuge&lt;/a&gt; indicates a rich avian population, including vultures and eagles. Along the rivers, you might encounter otters, various waterfowl, and other small mammals.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Fuentenebro?
A: While the trails themselves are primarily natural, the villages surrounding Fuentenebro, such as Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela and Valdevacas de Montejo, often have small local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal after your hike. It's always a good idea to carry water and snacks, especially on longer routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fuentenebro?
A: The hiking trails in Fuentenebro are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, particularly the scenic river gorges and the tranquility of the natural park areas.

Q: Are there options for long-distance hikes around Fuentenebro?
A: While Fuentenebro is known for its day hikes, some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036582116/natural-park-of-the-hoces-de-rio-riaza-circular-from-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt; (8.3 miles / 13.3 km), offer a substantial distance. For multi-day adventures, you might consider connecting several routes or exploring longer sections of the Riaza River trails, though dedicated long-distance paths are less common.
tips: Town of Burgos that was repopulated in the 10th century due to its location next to the Duero River, in the Riaza basin. In the area called "La Peña", marine fossils can be found, evidencing that all these lands were covered millions and millions of years ago by a sea.
Next to a meadow with tables, several barbecues and a shelter to shelter from the rain, we find the Aguacae waterfall next to a fountain (it does not always carry water).
